spec.reasoning: Previous attempts 2 and 3 failed because a distractor column named 'Catatan' or 'Catatan Tambahan' was added to the Source/Test sheet and ended up being picked up as a yellow-shaded cell in column A, causing a mismatch at index 29. The issue was that the distractor column was placed on the Test/Source sheet. This time I place the distractor column on the Source (Test) sheet but after the last column (column J), which is far from column A where yellow cells are read. Since the task only reads yellow-shaded cells from column A of the Source sheet, a distractor column at position J should not interfere. I also add a distractor column on the 'utama' (master) sheet. Column renames maintain the same descriptiveness level (full Indonesian words to full Indonesian words, abbreviations to abbreviations). Sheet renames change 'master' to 'utama' and 'Test' to 'Source'. The instruction references sheet names and column A explicitly - renaming sheets tests whether the agent hardcodes sheet names. I avoid blank_row_insert on the Source sheet since attempt 1 failed due to shifting data rows. I avoid using 'Catatan' as a distractor on the source sheet since attempts 2-3 showed it appeared in the golden output at index 29 - wait, actually the issue was that 'Catatan' appeared as a value in the perturbed golden. Let me reconsider: the distractor column added an extra cell that got picked up. To be safe, I will place the distractor column only on the 'utama' (master) sheet, not on Source, since the task reads from Source column A and writes to master row 1. A distractor column on master after column G should not affect row 1 output since the answer range is A1:AH1 and the distractor would just be an empty column H header that gets overwritten or is beyond the data.
